content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE
        Router reason: This is durable documentation of memory-system procedures and schemas, suitable as reusable note-level knowledge rather than a user profile/task update.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create a task unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Note Reconciler v2 lifecycle decision policy; In 03. Prompt Pack section 5, Note Reconciler v2 runs after NoteConstructor to compare candidate notes with nearby notes and choose lifecycle actions (`insert | update | supersede | retract | noop`), with rules to prefer `noop` for duplicates, `update` for clearer same-meaning notes, and `supersede` when replacing prior direction while preserving rationale via links.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 5 (Note Reconciler v2) in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Profile Updater v3 trigger and compact profile projection contract; In 03. Prompt Pack section 8, Profile Updater v3 is called after claim/task updates (sometimes after note consolidation) to rebuild a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context, returning strict JSON with profile_json, profile_text, and reason.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / 8. Profile Updater v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Claim Extractor v3 trigger and atomic-claim contract; In 03. Prompt Pack section 6, Claim Extractor v3 is invoked when the router chooses `direct_structured_write` or `mixed` (and can also be called by background consolidator) to extract atomic facts into a strict JSON claim schema with entity links, scope/qualifiers, confidence/importance, temporal validity, and reason.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / 6. Claim Extractor v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Claim Reconciler v3 decision policy for claim lifecycle ops; In 03. Prompt Pack section 7, Claim Reconciler v3 runs after ClaimExtractor and returns claim update operations (`insert | update | supersede | retract | noop`) using predicate coexist/replace/range rules, preferring supersede over silent overwrite and retract only for clear contradiction.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / 7. Claim Reconciler v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Task Updater v3 invocation policy and task lifecycle action schema; In 03. Prompt Pack section 9, Task Updater v3 runs when turns include commitments, deadlines, or task lifecycle updates, and returns a JSON array of task lifecycle operations (insert/update/close/cancel/noop) with task fields, reasons, and rules to avoid treating every request as commitment, normalize relative deadlines, and prefer one clear task.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / 9. Task Updater v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Prompt Pack memory prompts require strict input envelope and output validation; In 03. Prompt Pack, all memory prompts must receive a standard context envelope (time, timezone, namespace, source/turn selection, and optional retrieval/claim metadata), use structured JSON outputs with low temperature, and enforce strict validation with retry on invalid JSON.; scope=Applies to 03. Prompt Pack rules in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md section 0 (general rules).
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; Entity Canonicalizer v1 resolution policy and output actions; Entity Canonicalizer v1 runs after retrieval of candidate entities/aliases and resolves mentions by choosing among link_existing, create_new, add_alias, or noop, with a precision-first policy that avoids weak merges and only creates new entities when no safe match exists.; scope=03. Prompt Pack section '3. Entity Canonicalizer v1' in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<7> DOC_DIGEST; Note Constructor v2 role, trigger, and output contract; In 03. Prompt Pack section 4, Note Constructor v2 is invoked when router selects `note_write` or `mixed` and produces reusable semantic note candidates as structured JSON notes (with type, scope, entities, confidence/importance, maturity hint, and rationale), favoring few strong self-contained fragments over weak paraphrases.; scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 4 (Note Constructor v2) in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<8> DOC_DIGEST; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 invocation point and retrieval-plan contract; In 03. Prompt Pack, Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 is called after the router and before extraction/reconciliation to produce a precise retrieval plan focused on deduplication, contradiction detection, temporal updates, and scope alignment, with a strict JSON schema including query lists, memory types, time filters, and limits.; scope=03. Prompt Pack section '2. Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3' in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
- note hot-path:note:<9> DOC_DIGEST; Memory Router v3 decision policy and output contract; Memory Router v3 runs immediately after saving a Source and decides whether to start memory write plus mode selection (`noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, `mixed`) with declared memory types, salience, and short reason; policy emphasizes avoiding memory pollution and mapping content type to mode.; scope=Behavior contract for Memory Router v3 as defined in Prompt Pack section 1.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=03. Prompt Pack; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=03. Prompt Pack; reason=Exact match to existing document title and context.
- mention=agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md; reason=Exact match to existing file path in source metadata.
- mention=Note Consolidator v2; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Note Consolidator v2; reason=Mentioned as a distinct prompt/module and no existing candidate with this name.
- mention=Memory Repair Planner v1; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=Memory Repair Planner v1; reason=Mentioned as a distinct prompt/module and no existing candidate with this name.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=Note Consolidator v2|Memory Repair Planner v1|03. Prompt Pack|agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
        text_queries=Note Consolidator v2 prompt schema claim_candidates task_actions profile_patch episode_candidates note_actions summary|Memory Repair Planner v1 repair_actions merge_duplicates supersede_item archive_item downgrade_retrieval_state refresh_profile noop|durable structured memory consolidation rules promote to claim task episode|suspicious clusters memory repair conservative actions preserve history
        predicate_hints=

## 10. Note Consolidator v2

### Когда вызывать
В фоне, после debounce или batch window.

### Цель
Понять, можно ли mature notes превратить в durable structured memory.

### Выход

```json
{
  "claim_candidates": [],
  "task_actions": [],
  "profile_patch": null,
  "episode_candidates": [],
  "note_actions": [],
  "summary": "short summary"
}
```

### Prompt

```text
You are NoteConsolidator v2.

Goal:
Review mature notes and decide whether any of them should now produce durable structured memory.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Timezone: {{timezone}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Selected notes:
{{selected_notes}}

Related active claims:
{{related_claims}}

Related tasks:
{{related_tasks}}

Existing profile:
{{existing_profile_json}}

Return JSON:
{
  "claim_candidates": [],
  "task_actions": [],
  "profile_patch": null,
  "episode_candidates": [],
  "note_actions": [
    {
      "note_id": "uuid",
      "action": "keep_active | mark_resolved | mark_stale | supersede | mark_consolidated",
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "short summary"
}

Rules:
- Consolidate only when the signal is strong enough.
- Do not force every note into a claim.
- Promote to claim when the meaning is stable, queryable, and precise enough.
- Promote to task only when future action is clearly required.
- Promote to episode only when there is a reusable lesson.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

## 11. Memory Repair Planner v1

### Когда вызывать
В фоне, для suspicious clusters или после prompt/model changes.

### Цель
Предложить repair ops для деградировавшей памяти.

### Выход

```json
{
  "repair_actions": [
    {
      "action": "merge_duplicates | supersede_item | archive_item | downgrade_retrieval_state | refresh_profile | noop",
      "target_type": "note | claim | task | profile | entity",
      "target_ids": ["uuid"],
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "short summary"
}
```

### Prompt

```text
You are MemoryRepairPlanner v1.

Goal:
Review suspicious memory clusters and propose conservative repair actions that improve memory hygiene without destroying useful history.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Suspicious items:
{{suspicious_items}}

Supporting evidence:
{{supporting_evidence}}

Return JSON:
{
  "repair_actions": [
    {
      "action": "merge_duplicates | supersede_item | archive_item | downgrade_retrieval_state | refresh_profile | noop",
      "target_type": "note | claim | task | profile | entity",
      "target_ids": ["uuid"],
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "short summary"
}

Rules:
- Be conservative.
- Prefer preserving history with links/status changes over destructive edits.
- Use archive/downgrade when an item is stale but still explainable.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"notes":[{"candidate_claim_hints":[],"confidence":0.95,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<2>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"secondary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<1>","role":"secondary"}],"evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"- Consolidate only when the signal is strong enough.\n- Do not force every note into a claim.","evidence_reason":"These explicit rules define the module’s consolidation threshold and conservative promotion behavior.","importance":4,"keywords":["Note Consolidator v2","mature notes","claim_candidates","note_actions"],"note_type":"doc_digest","rationale":"This is reusable procedural policy and decision logic (when and how to promote notes), not a single atomic fact.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"document","text":"03. Prompt Pack / 10. Note Consolidator v2 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md"},"summary":"In 03. Prompt Pack section 10, Note Consolidator v2 runs in the background after debounce/batch windows to review mature notes and optionally promote them into durable memory outputs (claims, tasks, episodes, profile patches) only when signal is strong; it must not force conversion of every note.","tags":["prompt_pack","memory_consolidation","conservative_policy"],"title":"Note Consolidator v2 promotes mature notes to structured memory conservatively","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null},{"candidate_claim_hints":[],"confidence":0.95,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<3>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"secondary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<1>","role":"secondary"}],"evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"Review suspicious memory clusters and propose conservative repair actions that improve memory hygiene without destroying useful history.","evidence_reason":"This sentence directly states invocation context and the governing repair philosophy.","importance":4,"keywords":["Memory Repair Planner v1","repair_actions","suspicious clusters","preserve history"],"note_type":"doc_digest","rationale":"The section captures durable troubleshooting/maintenance strategy and action schema, which is better stored as a reusable note than as isolated claims.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"document","text":"03. Prompt Pack / 11. Memory Repair Planner v1 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md"},"summary":"In 03. Prompt Pack section 11, Memory Repair Planner v1 is invoked for suspicious clusters or after prompt/model changes, and proposes conservative repair operations (e.g., merge_duplicates, supersede_item, archive_item, downgrade_retrieval_state, refresh_profile, noop) to improve memory hygiene while preserving explainable history.","tags":["prompt_pack","memory_repair","hygiene"],"title":"Memory Repair Planner v1 uses conservative, history-preserving repair actions","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null}]}
